title: Workflow-management wire service (start/signal/query/cancel/list/describe)
purpose: Define the gRPC service and request/response messages for the core workflow-management surface every caller uses. This is the wire form of the engine's start/signal/query/cancel/list/describe API — the contract client SDKs generate stubs from.
task: Add workflow.proto declaring a service with StartWorkflow, Signal, Query, Cancel, ListWorkflows, and DescribeWorkflow RPCs and their request/response messages. Each request carries a namespace; inputs and results are aion-core Payload; ListWorkflows carries a WorkflowFilter and returns WorkflowSummary values; DescribeWorkflow returns a summary (and optionally history). Provide the serde request/response types and assert lossless proto<->serde round-trips.

requirements.title: Workflow service definition
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL define a gRPC service in workflow.proto with StartWorkflow, Signal, Query, Cancel, ListWorkflows, and DescribeWorkflow RPCs, each with a request and a response message.
acceptance: workflow.proto defines a service exposing all six RPCs.
StartWorkflow request carries namespace, workflow type, and input Payload; response carries the assigned WorkflowId and RunId.
Signal request carries namespace, target WorkflowId, signal name, and payload Payload; Cancel request carries namespace, WorkflowId, and reason.
Query request carries namespace, WorkflowId, and query name; response carries a result Payload or a WireError.
tonic-build generates the service stub and the crate compiles.

requirements.title: Namespace, Payload, Filter, and Summary on the wire
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL carry a namespace on every request, carry inputs/results as aion-core Payload, and carry ListWorkflows as a WorkflowFilter returning WorkflowSummary values.
acceptance: Every request message in workflow.proto has a namespace field.
Inputs and results use the common Payload message (from AW-002), not bespoke byte fields.
ListWorkflows request carries a WorkflowFilter (via the AW-002 envelope/conversion) and its response carries a repeated WorkflowSummary.
DescribeWorkflow response carries a WorkflowSummary (history inclusion is optional and, if present, carried as repeated Event envelopes).

requirements.title: Serde types and round-trip
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL provide serde request/response types for the workflow-management messages that derive Serialize + Deserialize and round-trip losslessly to and from their proto form.
acceptance: Each workflow-management request/response has a serde-deriving Rust type (the generated prost type or a hand-paired serde type per the crate's chosen pattern).
A unit test round-trips at least StartWorkflow, ListWorkflows, and Query request+response through serde_json and through proto encode/decode, asserting equality.
All wire data types here derive both Serialize and Deserialize (CO7) — none is Deserialize-only (these are data, not credentials).

boundaries: SHALL NOT define event-streaming or worker messages — AW-004, AW-005.
SHALL NOT implement the server handlers that call the Engine — that is AW-008.
SHALL NOT add server-side orchestration semantics; this brief defines the wire shape only.
verification: No #[allow], #[expect], or #[ignore] directives in new code.
